title: Coconut wax jar candle
subtitle: A creamy white container candle with a high fragrance load
excerpt: A coconut wax container candle in a 200 ml jar with a 10% fragrance load. Coconut wax is the softest natural wax, with the best fragrance-binding capacity of any natural wax; it holds up to 12% fragrance oil without sweating. The candle burns cleanly with a strong hot throw.
sourceNotes: CandleScience coconut wax notes (candlescience.com, May 2026), fragrance load capacity and pour temperature. Bramble Berry coconut wax guide (brambleberry.com, May 2026), wick sizing notes.

content.text: A coconut wax container candle. Coconut wax is pressed from coconut meat and has the softest texture and highest fragrance-binding capacity of the natural waxes. At 10% fragrance load (the high end for natural waxes), the candle has a very strong hot throw when burning. The wax is naturally creamy white.

than soy wax (around 45°C) and stays soft for longer after pouring; allow 48 hours cure before burning. In a warm room above 25°C the candle surface may stay slightly tacky; this is normal and does not affect burn quality.
Melt wax on a simmering-water bath, not directly on the hob. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach.

items.name: Soy wax (container blend)
items.prepNote: coconut wax (container grade). Substitute soy wax if coconut wax is unavailable, and reduce fragrance load to 8%.

items.name: Candle fragrance oil
items.prepNote: fragrance oil or essential oil of choice. 10% of the wax weight. Coconut wax accepts this load without sweating. Add at 50°C.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: pre-tabbed cotton wick sized for a 7 cm diameter jar in coconut wax (typically one size larger than soy for the same jar diameter).

content.text: Adhere the wick tab to the centre base of the jar. Thread the wick through the centring bar laid across the top of the jar.

Melt the coconut wax in the pouring pitcher over a simmering-water bath. Remove from the heat and allow to cool. When the probe reads 50°C, stir in the fragrance oil and mix for 2 minutes. When the wax cools to 50-55°C, pour steadily into the jar. The
for coconut wax is lower than soy; pouring too hot causes a sticky surface on the finished candle. Leave undisturbed for 48 hours.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. Allow the first burn to run 3-4 hours for the full

Effectively indefinite unlit. Store in a cool location; coconut wax stays softer than other natural waxes and a warm storage location above 25°C will cause the candle surface to remain tacky and attract dust. Burn within 2 years for best scent throw.
